Subject: 00UTO00000YQwCV2A1
Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Met with Joe to discuss his current usage, address his concerns regarding our approval rates compared to SNAP, and provide a refresher on tax requirements.

Meeting Notes:

Account Status & Positioning: Joe is actively using our program and expressed a desire to use us more because of my local presence and support.

Competitor Intel & Pushback: He is frustrated that our approval amounts are consistently lower than SNAP's, and noted that we frequently decline customers who SNAP ends up approving.

Objection Handling: Acknowledged his concerns regarding the approval differences and assured him that we are aware of the issue and actively working on correcting it.

Training & Compliance: Reviewed the specific tax requirements associated with the EasyPay program, explaining the reasoning behind them based on our structure as a Lease-to-Own (LTO) product.

Subject: 00UTO00000YSsQ52AL
Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Met with Jay, Dallas, and AJ to update system access and discuss current financing strategies. Brought breakfast for the team to build rapport and encourage program usage.
Meeting Notes: Successfully created new logins for the team and removed old users who are no longer at the location. AJ is the District Manager overseeing the shop, while Dallas and Jay act as the service writers. Although Dallas and Jay are very familiar with EasyPay, the location's current process relies heavily on Formpiper as their primary financing option, which we are not integrated into.
Both service writers agreed to try pushing our program more, though they noted that recent customer declines have made it difficult to fully advocate for us right now. Bringing breakfast was a step toward overcoming this pushback and re-establishing our presence as a viable option for their customers

Subject: 00UTO00000YViAr2AL
Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Training with Amir has been successfully completed. During the session, we reviewed the requirement that all transactions must be processed in person and that customer ID verification is mandatory. It was explicitly communicated that we cannot cover downpayments on any transaction. We also discussed that EasyPay will eventually audit shop invoices, so it is crucial that the invoices he writes accurately detail what EasyPay is covering and that the descriptions match our records. Amir acknowledged that we charge taxes on our end and stated this is perfectly fine with him. Furthermore, he expressed appreciation that we do not charge any merchant fees, noting that this is a strong advantage over SNAP, which charges him a 3% fee and currently serves as his default first option.  - Had Noemy create credentials for Amir to login. Made aware that he can create his profile and start using the program.
_label_Full Comments:

Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Checked in via Maps Application

Account/Contact: John – AAMCO (La Cienega)
Interaction Type: Follow-Up Visit

Summary:
Visited AAMCO on La Cienega and had the opportunity to speak with John. He shared that they currently use EasyPay as a secondary option, typically when customers indicate they may not qualify for traditional financing.

He noted that recently, most customers have been paying with cash or choosing not to move forward with repairs, which has limited financing opportunities.

Reinforced the value of EasyPay by explaining the 90-day same-as-cash option, including the $40 processing fee if carried the full term, and clarified that interest only applies to any remaining balance after the 90-day period. This positioning helps address affordability concerns and can prevent lost deals.

Key Insights:
• EasyPay used as secondary option
• Customers primarily paying cash or delaying repairs
• Opportunity to position financing earlier in the sales...

Visited location and met with the owner. He shared that he prefers to use EasyPay Finance as his primary option and uses Snap only as a backup.

Shop has four bays, with an employee working in each bay at the time of visit, indicating steady workflow.

Identified potential opportunity to open a second account for South Bay Tires. Will follow up on next visit to discuss setup.

Plan is to follow up and continue reinforcing primary usage while exploring second account opportunity.

Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Salesforce Recap

Contact Name: Andrew (Shop Manager)

Observations:  
Spoke with Andrew, the shop manager, who shared that they currently use Snap Finance, our product, and other financing options. Overall financing activity has been slow, with most customers opting to pay via credit cards. The location has strong marketing presence, including our banners on the windows and materials in-store, alongside visible competitor branding. Conversation focused on understanding current challenges and reinforcing support.

Feedback:  
- Financing usage is slow due to customer preference for credit cards  
- Strong in-store marketing presence for both our product and competitors  
- Andrew was open and communicative; values support and engagement  

Next Steps:

Account Visit – West General Auto Body Repair

Stopped by location to connect with Latrice, but she was not available. Spoke with Rochelle, the office manager, instead. She shared that they sometimes use Snap and noted Snap marketing has been more prominent since last year.

Rochelle mentioned that many customers attempt to apply with EasyPay but are not getting approved. I reviewed common application issues, including customers using accounts that do not verify identification (such as some fintech accounts like Chime) which may result in auto-decline, and clarified that a checking account is required, as savings accounts can also lead to rejection.

Introduced myself as the local representative and positioned myself as a resource for application guidance and support. Plan to follow up with Latrice directly and continue working to improve approval success and usage.

Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 

I spoke with Dahlia. She mentioned that they have attempted to use our service, but have found it challenging for their customer base. Many of their customers are either non-English speakers or elderly, which makes the application process more difficult.

She explained that Snap is easier for them because it allows staff to complete the application in-store on behalf of the customer. In contrast, our process requires customers to call in and answer questions themselves, which can be difficult for older customers or those with language barriers.

As a result, they find Snap more convenient and easier to use in their day-to-day operations.

Checked in via Maps Application 
Salesforce Recap

Contact Name: Nick (Front Desk Associate)

Observations:  
Spoke with a front desk associate believed to be Nick. He shared that the majority of their customer base is from the Asian community, and the business primarily operates on a cash-and-carry basis. As a result, they have moved away from regularly using EasyPay. Given this context, financing is not a strong focus at this location, which aligns with current usage trends.

Feedback:  
- Low usage of EasyPay due to customer payment preferences (cash-based transactions)  
- Limited opportunity for financing growth under current business model  
- Low overall potential as a high-performing merchant  

Next Steps:  
- Deprioritize account for now  
- Periodically check in to see if business needs or customer behavior changes  
- Maintain light-touch relationship for any future opportunities
